title: Questions to Ask During a Web Designer Interview
excerpt: Choosing the right web designer often comes down to the conversation before any work begins. These interview questions help you evaluate not just technical skill, but process, professionalism, and fit — so you can hire with confidence.
content:
A portfolio tells you what someone has done. An interview tells you who they are and how they work. Here are the questions that reveal the most.

About Their Process

  • "Can you walk me through how you approach a new web design project from start to finish?"
  • "How do you begin the discovery phase, and what information do you need from a client?"
  • "How do you handle wireframing — do you always wireframe before visual design?"
What you're looking for: A structured, repeatable process that includes discovery, strategy, wireframing, and testing. Designers who skip straight to visual design often miss strategic opportunities.

About Communication and Collaboration

  • "How often would we communicate during a project, and through what channels?"
  • "How do you present design concepts — do you walk clients through your thinking?"
  • "How do you handle disagreements about a design direction?"
What you're looking for: Proactive communication, openness to collaboration, and the ability to explain design decisions in non-technical language.

About Revisions and Scope

  • "How many revision rounds are included in your proposal?"
  • "What happens if I request changes outside the agreed scope?"
  • "Can you give me an example of how you've managed scope creep on a previous project?"

About Technical Skills

  • "What platforms and CMSes do you typically build on, and why?"
  • "How do you ensure your designs are optimized for page speed and SEO?"
  • "Do you work with developers, or do you handle development yourself?"

About Their Track Record

  • "Can I speak with a previous client whose project was similar to mine?"
  • "Have you ever had a project go wrong? How did you handle it?"

Partner With Professionals
